Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-11-2022 Continued To Apr-05-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Knitter, via Zoom, for William Ribeiro, Petitoner; John Vaught, via Zoom, for Victora Noon, Respondent.

The court points to probate code 48 and that it has no standing in a 17200 petition. Mr. Knitter offers advocacy on behalf of his position. The court advises of its position and being bound by the probate code and case law with regards to actual and specific intent. Mr. Knitter and the court discuss Ana Ribeiro's estate with Mr. Knitter advising that he is uncertain that probate has been opened. Mr. Ribeiro advises of an existing will for Ana. The court advises that notice will be required to the beneficiaries of the will.

After additional discussion, including the court encouraging the parties to consider settlement discussions, the matter is continued to Apr-05-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises that it intends to issue a Case Management Order.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Court Reporter: Diane Wheeler CSR#: 5237 T: 916.803.3083 E: diane.wheeler.csr@gmail.com.

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-23-2021 Continued To Jan-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Sean Culligan, Esq. for and with William Ribeiro;
Jon Vaught, Esq. for Victoria Noon.

The Court stated that notice was not on the mandatory Judicial Council form. Mr. Culligan states that notice was given to all parties. The Court and counsel confer regarding the date that notice was given. The Court addresses Mr. Culligan noting that he needs to follow the rules, and that there should be direct notice to Ms. Noon unless Mr. Vaught accepts service. The Court notes that all parties are not present to waive notice. The Court states that now that the petition is on file that Mr. Culligan should give everyone notice. Mr. Culligan apologized to the Court for the delay.

Mr. Vaught states that his client will waive notice. Mr. Culligan asks that the minutes reflect that Mr. Vaught will accept service on behalf of his client.

At the conclusion of counsels' comments, the Court continued the Amended Petition for Reformation of Trust; Request for Evidentiary Hearing to Tuesday, January 4, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court states that notice is critical.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Chan. Court Reporter: John Fahrenwald, CSR #14369, Tel. 520.861.2450, Email: John.Fahrenwald@gmail.com.

After the hearing, the matter was reset to January 11, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties were notified of the new date by the probate examiner.

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Continued To Nov-23-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-10-2021 Continued To Oct-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught, via ZOOM, for Victoria Noon, Petitioner; David Knitter, via ZOOM, for William Ribeiro, Objector.

The court begins by summarizing the proceedings at the previous calling of this matter. The court then references the case of the Estate of Duke and the Anti-Lapse statute and advises that the court doesn't believe the petition can proceed as-is and may need to be amended. Both Mr. Vaught and Mr. Knitter provide oral testimony to support their positions with Mr. Vaught advising that he has not spoken to the attorney who drafted the trust instrument. The court advises Mr. Vaught that he should consider discussing the trust with the attorney who drafted the trust prior to filing an amended petition.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Reformation, etc., is continued to October 5, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. If an amended petition is filed, it should be filed with the court by no later than September 23, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Court Reporter: Lisa Kennedy CSR: 8927 T: 916.7612082 E: lgkreporter@aol.com.

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-21-2021 Continued To Aug-10-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught, via ZOOM, for Victoria Noon, Petitioner; David Knitter, via ZOOM, for William Ribeiro, Objector.

The court at the outset confirms with Mr. Knitter of a pending filing in the court's electronic filing queue. Mr. Knitter then provides oral argument in support of his petition and requests that the allow him an evidentiary hearing. The court advises Mr. Knitter that it is not clear as to the theory of his petition. Mr. Knitter provides additional oral argument. Mr. Vaught then provides oral testimony to support his position including the anti-lapse statute and for denial of Mr. Ribeiro's petition.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Reformation or In the Alternative Termination of Trust is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Continued To Jul-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Advanced From May-26-21 To Apr-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar May-26-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by zoom for Victoria Noon (Petitioner).
Sean Culligan appeared by zoom for and with William Ribeiro (Petitioner) by zoom.

The Court stated that it read everything and that its tentative is based on the facts of this case and the anti-lapse statute, Probate Code 21110. The Court also stated that the anti-lapse statute provides in situations like this where there is a Beneficiary and the Beneficiary dies, it is presumed to go to the Beneficiary's issue unless there is a contrary intent in the trust instrument, that the Court sees this as a gift to Victoria's Father and that under a provision of PC 21110 it presumptively goes to Victoria. In addition, the Court stated that none of what's in the objection goes to what's in the trust instrument, and that its intent is to grant Victoria's petition and deny William's petition. Mr. Culligan stated that there is a notice issue, that they have to notify all of the interested parties, and requested a continuance to locate Anne's children. Mr. Vaught objects to a continuance stating that his petition is properly noticed and has been before the Court for months, and that the only procedural issue is with William's petition. Mr. Culligan stated that he would like a continuance to conduct discovery and present extrinsic evidence. The Court noted that the problem is that the intent has to be in the trust instrument and questions what's ambiguous. Mr. Culligan stated that it's not a complete trust instrument because it does not show what should happen and what the Trustor's intent was. The Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee (Victoria's petition) is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition. The Petition for Reformation or in the Alternative Termination of Trust (William's petition) is continued to July 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar May-25-2021 Continued To Jul-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Continued To May-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Reformation Or In The Alternative Termination Of Trust
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Continued To May-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application For Order To Advance Hearing To April 20, 2021
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-16-2021 Continued To Apr-20-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by zoom for and with Victoria Noon (Petitioner) by zoom.
Sean Culligan appeared by zoom for and with William Ribeiro (Objector) by zoom.

Mr. Culligan stated he filed a cross-petition, that he is objecting to the appointment of Victoria Noon as the Successor Trustee and to her being the only Heir, and that he would like to do some discovery. Mr. Culligan also stated that the trust instrument is not clear, that there is no successor trustee provision, referred to the Estate of Duke and stated that he believes extrinsic evidence could come in to show the intent of the Settlor. Mr. Vaught raised the issue of whether the anti-slapp statute is applicable, questions what authority Mr. Culligan has, stated that William Ribeiro is not a Beneficiary of the trust and that Mr. Ribeiro has no standing. Mr. Vaught also stated that the Settlor created two trusts, that one was for John and one was for William, that the trust for John does not leave anything to William and that the trust for William does not leave anything to John. In addition, Mr. Vaught stated that no one is currently managing the property in San Francisco, and that they have a person living in the property with no lease and no rent. Mr. Ribeiro stated that Ms. Noon was estranged from the family and that there is no way his Father would have wanted her to have the house or be the Trustee. The Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee is continued to April 20, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Mr. Culligan that once he gets a hearing date on his petition, he is to move it to April 20th.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Continued To Mar-16-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-27-2021 Continued To Feb-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jon Vaught appeared by zoom for Petitioner.

The Court stated that it is inclined to order notice to William and Anne or their issue, and asked Counsel if he has any information on the Wife. Mr. Vaught stated that he has no information on them, that he thinks William and Anne are still alive and that he believes the Settlor was divorced. The Court stated that there then needs to be notice to their issue or information on whether they have no issue, that it is ordering notice to them because they are mentioned in the trust and that they may have more information regarding the family. Mr. Vaught stated that the trust didn't leave anything to anyone of these persons, and that they may have an interest in the estate, but not the trust. The Court stated that Mr. Vaught's Client will need to be bonded and that he is to do due diligence on the issue of bond. Mr. Vaught stated that his Client is the sole beneficiary of the trust. The Court noted that the trust does not waive bond for anyone other than John. Mr. Vaught read language of the trust, spoke of the concerns in the Probate Examiner's notes and the request for proof of death and whether there are any other children, stated that sometimes people have children out of wedlock and may not know it, and asked how someone can prove that there are no other children. The Court stated that it is not concerned about proof of death, but it wants something broader regarding the issue of other children. The Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee is continued to February 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
